recent_changes_summary: v0.8.0 stable cut on 2026-05-01 — Wave 1 + Wave 2 (AP-2, AP-3, AP-4 routing, AP-5, AP-6).
AP-4 wire-up (e88482d): replaced ap_sweep.py's 4 stubbed call sites with real Gmail / Drive / Chat API integrations. Wave 2 fully operational.

AP-7 (4cf1170): labor_ingest.py — StaffWizard Overall Report parser, project grouping via project_registry.resolve_by_staffwizard_job, per-project daily labor workbook writer. Real-data validation: 108 April 29 shifts → 28 project groups.
AP-8 (a59f119): master_rollup.py — three-tab workbook (All Projects, PM Dashboard, Anomalies). N=30-day cold-start baseline, ±2σ deviation alerts in both directions, 7d/30d rolling run-rates.

AR-9 (54c69d9): ar_invoicing.py — invoice generation from labor (grouped by post), Net-15 default + per-customer overrides, weekly cadence for NY projects, 6 aging buckets, 5-tier collections cadence (no double-send).
AR-9 send wire-up: ar_send.py — HTML email body + Excel attachment for invoices, tier-appropriate reminder templates, dispatched via Gmail API. Calls mark_sent / add_collection_event on success.

Wave 3 MCP wrappers (tools/ap_wave3.py): 10 new tools (workflow_ingest_labor_report, workflow_build_master_rollup, workflow_generate_customer_invoice, workflow_invoice_mark_sent / apply_payment, workflow_ar_aging_report, workflow_collections_due_today, workflow_send_invoice, workflow_send_collection_reminder, workflow_record_daily_fact).
Total since v0.8.0: 4 new modules + 10 new MCP tools + ~59 new unit tests + ~1,800 LOC.
pick_up_here: AP-1 needs the Workday GL → Spend Category map to unblock. Geotab needs API credentials. After those, P1-7 Slack and the broad-except polish backlog are the remaining items.
instructions_for_next_handler: Read HANDOFF_LOG.md latest entry first (2026-05-01 v0.8.1 cut).
Read coassisted-workspace-ap-roadmap-2026-04-30.md on the operator's Desktop for the AP/AR vision.
Bump VERSION in _version.py + pyproject.toml if you ship features (e.g. 0.8.2-dev).
Append a new entry to HANDOFF_LOG.md before handing off.
Run `make test-fast` and record pass/fail in your log entry.
Commit each spec as a discrete git commit with the spec id.
